segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: "Well,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a voice observed, dry as desert wind.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "That looks profoundly uncomfortable."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin spun, hand instinctively moving to the sword at his hip.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A woman leaned against the glass ridge, arms crossed, assessing him with the calm focus of a predator deciding if he was a threat or opportunity. Twin daggers rested at her hips, their hilts worn smooth with use.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her travel cloak was torn and stained with ash, and one eye was green while the other shone gold, catching the light like a cat's.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her expression suggested she found the entire situation darkly amusing.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: "Virella Sunshadow,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, not moving from her perch.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Though people who haven't tried to murder me yet generally call me Vex."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "How long have you been there?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin asked, still catching his breath from the bonding.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Long enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She pushed off from the stone and slid down the glass slope with practiced ease, boots finding purchase where he would have slipped.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Long enough to watch a dragon die passing something to a stranger. Long enough to see that very permanent addition to your arm."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mismatched gaze dropped to the embedded scale, studying it with professional interest.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Interesting accessory. Not exactly subtle."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin instinctively tried to cover it with his other hand, then stopped. What was the point? The scale was flush with his skin, inlaid like jewelry he could never remove.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You shouldn't be here. This is -- "

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Dangerous? Obviously."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex stopped a few feet away, close enough to talk comfortably, far enough to react if he proved hostile.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "But I've been tracking something dangerous for months now, so the ambiance feels appropriate."

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Tracking what?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Answers."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She pulled a water-stained journal from inside her cloak and held it up briefly before tucking it away again.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "My mother was a scholar. Obsessed with old alliances, ancient pacts, something she called 'fire-kept covenants.' She died six months ago in the Emberpeaks, and all I have left are her research notes and more questions than sense."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex gestured at the dragon's crystalline corpse.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Her last journal entry mentioned rumors of a dragon searching for something. That was three weeks before she disappeared. So when I heard about a dragon burning villages in a very specific pattern -- not raiding, not feeding, but searching -- I followed the trail."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her eyes met his.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "And here I find not treasure, but you. With that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A nod at his arm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm against his skin, but didn't sting. Whatever else Vex might be hiding, she was being honest about her motivations.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Your mother was looking for the same thing the dragon gave me,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said slowly.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Was she?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's tone sharpened with interest.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "And what exactly did that dying dragon give you, besides permanent body modification?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He hesitated. The scale warmed in warning -- truth or pain, choose.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "A burden. A purpose. A path to follow."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "How poetically vague."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But her eyes tracked to something behind him, and her expression shifted.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "What are you staring at?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin glanced back at the flame-thread stretching toward the mountains, then returned his attention to her.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You can't see it?"

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "See what?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex followed his gaze, squinting at empty air above the crater's rim.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "All I'm getting is heat shimmer and the worst headache I've had in months. Which, given my lifestyle, is saying something."

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "There's a path. Fire, but not fire. Leading into the Emberpeaks."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He flexed his marked arm, feeling the scale pulse in time with the distant thread.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The dragon said only those marked could see it."

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Of course she did."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex rubbed her temples.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Let me guess -- this invisible magic road leads straight into the mountains where my mother vanished. Where the last three caravans disappeared without a trace. Where absolutely nothing good has been heard from in half a year."

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "Probably."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Perfect."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She let her hands drop and fixed him with an evaluating look.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: "New arrangement, then. You need someone watching your back while you follow your glowing destiny path. I need someone who can actually see where we're going and potentially lead me to what my mother died seeking."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin raised an eyebrow.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You walk in front,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said with the practicality of someone who'd survived on the margins,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "drawing every monster's attention with that beacon in your arm. I follow behind, keeping us both alive with my actually useful skills like not getting eaten. Meanwhile, we both get closer to our answers."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's your pitch? I'm bait?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Mutually beneficial bait."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mouth quirked.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Look, you're going into those mountains whether I come or not -- I can see it in your face. That scale's already made the choice for you. And I'm going regardless of whether you want company, because my mother's last camp was somewhere in those peaks and I'm not leaving until I know what happened to her."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She crossed her arms.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "So we can stumble into whatever's waiting separately and probably both die, or we can watch each other's backs and maybe one of us survives long enough to accomplish something. Your choice, ember-boy."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The nickname irritated him, but the logic was sound. And the scale wasn't stinging -- she meant what she said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You said your mother called them 'fire-kept covenants,'"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Did her notes mention anything about Nine? Or about something sealed?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's expression sharpened.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The Ninefold Concord. She had that phrase written everywhere in her later journals -- underlined, circled, with question marks. She thought it was some kind of alliance between dragons and... someone else. Dwarves, maybe. From before the kingdoms."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mismatched eyes narrowed.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Why?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Because the dragon spoke of Nine relics. Nine bearers. And something called the Tenth that has to stay sleeping."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The words felt too big, too important for the smoking crater and morning light.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Your mother was looking for the same thing I've just been bound to find."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a long moment, Vex was silent, her expression cycling through several emotions before settling on grim determination.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then I'm definitely coming with you. If the thing that killed my mother is connected to whatever's in your arm, I want to be there when we find it."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It might not have been a thing that killed her,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said quietly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The mountains are dangerous even without ancient conspiracies."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Maybe."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand drifted to one of her daggers, a habitual gesture that spoke of comfort rather than threat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "But she was too good to die to something ordinary. Too careful. If something in those peaks took her, I need to know what. And I need to know if it's still there."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed steady and warm. No sting. Truth spoken plainly.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We leave as soon as I can gather supplies from what's left of the village,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The path leads east toward the Emberpeaks. The dragon said there's a sanctum there -- the First Sanctum, she called it. That's where we're going."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's mouth twitched into something almost like a smile.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Look at that. Partnership established through mutual desperation and probable doom. My favorite kind."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I should warn you,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin added, looking down at the scale embedded in his arm, watching the ember-motes drift in its translucent depths.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "This thing has rules. It punishes lies. Forces truth. I'm not entirely in control of what I might say anymore."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Good,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, surprising him.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Lies get people killed. I'd rather travel with someone who can't lie than someone I have to constantly second-guess."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She glanced at the dragon's body, her expression softening slightly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Besides, after watching what just happened -- whatever that was -- I think we're past the point where normal rules apply."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stood in the crater's heart, the morning sun climbing higher, burning away the last of the smoke. The flame-path stretched toward distant peaks, visible only to Caelin's marked sight. The scale pulsed its steady rhythm against his skin.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We should go,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Whatever was drawn by the dragon's fire might still be nearby. And I'd rather not explain to the villagers why I was watching from the shadows while their savior got magically branded."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Fair point."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin took one last look at Vharisax -- already transforming into monument, into legend, into the glass-frozen history of this place.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Thank you,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said quietly, not entirely sure if he meant it for the burden or the purpose or simply for answering the question his entire life had been circling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dragon didn't answer. She'd said everything she needed to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed the crater's slope together, glass singing its low crystalline note beneath their boots. At the rim, Caelin paused, looking toward Thornwick's smoke and then toward the Emberpeaks' distant blue silhouette.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the dragon lay in her crater of transformed earth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the flame-path beckoned with patient, inexorable pull.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And on his arm, the scale kept its steady count of heartbeats, marking time until destiny could no longer be delayed.
